message:
perf(ingest): memoize wal_autocheckpoint pragma instead of bake-in

Replace the pragma-bake-in approach from commit 8c2c0880
(open_ephemeral_writer + apply_writable_config) with a per-handle
memoization of the current `wal_autocheckpoint` value. The bake-in
shape only avoided the duplicate pragma on the very first call —
every subsequent open of a short-lived writer paid the same
generic-config-then-override cost again because the new ephemeral
writer was a fresh handle. Memoization dodges the pragma entirely
on the long-lived storage handle whenever the requested value
already matches the cached one, which is the steady-state case
during a continuous index run.

Storage layer (`src/storage/sqlite.rs`):

- Drop `FrankenStorage::open_ephemeral_writer(...)` and the helper
  `apply_writable_config(busy_timeout_ms, wal_autocheckpoint_pages,
  foreign_keys_enabled)` that the prior commit introduced. Drop
  the `DEFAULT_CACHE_SIZE_KIB` and `DEFAULT_BUSY_TIMEOUT_MS`
  constants that only existed to feed those parameters; the
  pragmas inside `apply_config` go back to inline literals (cache
  size 64MB, busy timeout 5s, foreign keys ON).
- Add `index_writer_checkpoint_pages: AtomicI64` to
  `FrankenStorage`, initialized to a sentinel
  `UNSET_INDEX_WRITER_CHECKPOINT_PAGES = i64::MIN` in the
  `FrankenStorage::new` constructor. After `apply_config` issues
  its `PRAGMA wal_autocheckpoint = 1000`, store
  `DEFAULT_WAL_AUTOCHECKPOINT_PAGES` into the atomic so the cache
  reflects reality from the moment the handle is open.
- Add `pub(crate) index_writer_checkpoint_pages() -> Option<i64>`
  (returns `None` when the sentinel is still in place, otherwise
  the last value seen) and
  `mark_index_writer_checkpoint_pages(pages: i64)` to update it.
  These are deliberately `Relaxed` ordering — the value is purely
  advisory: a stale read just causes one extra pragma call, never
  wrong behavior.

Indexer layer (`src/indexer/mod.rs`):

- `apply_index_writer_checkpoint_policy(storage, defer_checkpoints)`
  short-circuits at the top: compute the target
  `wal_autocheckpoint_pages` and bail out immediately if
  `storage.index_writer_checkpoint_pages() == Some(target)`. The
  PRAGMA only fires when the cached value differs (or is unset).
  On success, call `storage.mark_index_writer_checkpoint_pages(...)`
  so the next call sees the fresh value and skips the round-trip.
- Revert `with_ephemeral_writer` to call `FrankenStorage::open_-
  writer(&db_path)` and re-apply the per-call
  `apply_index_writer_busy_timeout`, `apply_index_writer_-
  checkpoint_policy`, and `PRAGMA foreign_keys = OFF` overrides.
  These are cheap on the ephemeral writer (one short-lived
  connection, so memoization there isn't useful), and removing
  them in 8c2c0880 was masking a real concern: the bake-in path
  diverged the production open path from the open path the rest
  of the test suite uses, so a follow-up PRAGMA change had to be
  made in two places. Both `busy_timeout_ms` and
  `wal_autocheckpoint_pages` reads are now back inside
  `with_ephemeral_writer` (after the writer opens) so they're not
  computed twice.

Tests (`src/indexer/mod.rs`):

- `apply_index_writer_checkpoint_policy_*` test now also asserts
  `storage.index_writer_checkpoint_pages()`:
  - `Some(1000)` after `create_franken_db` (set by `apply_config`).
  - `Some(0)` after `apply_index_writer_checkpoint_policy(true)`.
- The `open_ephemeral_writer_applies_specialized_writer_pragmas`
  test introduced in 8c2c0880 is removed — `open_ephemeral_writer`
  no longer exists, and its assertions (FK off, busy_timeout=60s,
  wal_autocheckpoint=0) are already covered by the live behavior
  of `with_ephemeral_writer` plus the existing
  `with_ephemeral_writer_marks_preflight_verified_after_first_-
  success` test.

No behavior change visible to consumers; this is purely a cheaper
way to skip duplicate pragmas in the steady state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
